body_txt: Some characters just have it out for teenagers. Whether they see teens as delinquents up to no good, as monsters , sex fiends , or just plain bullies , adults - especially parents, the elderly, law enforcement, and middle/high school teachers - might despise teenagers for rebelling against their authority and being sassy little runts . At the same time, younger children might hold a grudge against teens, not just for being bullies but for simply having more freedom than they do, which they're very much jealous of. Even teenagers themselves are susceptible to self-loathing, due to negative experiences with and stereotypes surrounding their age group. It should be noted that adults who specifically hate teenagers and not younger children may favor the latter for being more innocent (or at least too young to fully know better), and parents who fall into this trope may feel nostalgia over when their rebellious teen Used to Be a Sweet Kid . Conversely, kids who specifically hate teens and not adults may be on the latter's side for attempting to discourage teens' misbehavior. Much of the fuel for this trope comes from the stigma around teenagers: Recklessness, cringey behavior, and immaturity are commonly associated with this age group, even though that isn't always the case . This trope generally portrays the Teen Haters sympathetically if the teens themselves are indeed monsters or, in less villainous cases, simply cruel kids . In other instances, the opposite route may be taken; by focusing on the teens' perspective, stories emerge of Troubled Teens whose destructive behavior results from a Freudian Excuse - a Dark and Troubled Past , problems at home , or just frustration over being expected to act like adults yet being treated like children (i.e. expected to shoulder responsibilities they may or may not be ready for, while simultaneously lacking any real access or privileges that would make those responsibilities easier). This may make the Teen Haters in question the antagonists, and in retaliation to a perceived lack of understanding from their elders, the teenagers may form a Teenage Wasteland . Compare Child Hater , the younger version of this trope, which may overlap if the work takes place in Junior High and has the early teens be more developmentally in line with preteens. In contrast, the Child Hater trope portrays its victims more sympathetically by using the belief that Children Are Innocent , though they can be portrayed just as equally unsympathetic as victims of this trope if the work insists that Kids Are Cruel . It must be said that there are people in real life who detest teenagers ; in fact, this phenomenon has been termed " ephebiphobia ". However, given the controversy of this subject, No Real Life Examples, Please! .
